source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure a support access group has been created to manage incidents with IBM Support

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure a support access group has been created to manage incidents with IBM Support
input_row.description: If you experience problems with IBM Cloud®, you can use support cases to get help with technical, access (IAM), billing & usage, and invoice or sales inquiry issues. You can create and manage a support case by using the Support Center. Access to Support Center is governed via IAM roles. In order to access Support Center, a user must have viewer or higher on the Support Center service. To create or edit service cases, a user must have editor or higher on the Support Center service. After you submit a support case, the support team investigates the issue based on your type of support plan. The types of available support depends on the support level of the account. Your support plan also determines the severity level that you can assign to support cases. For more information, see Case severity and response time.
input_row.rationale: Support cases are used to raise issues to IBM Cloud. Access to IBM Cloud Support Center is managed via IAM roles. IAM roles can be used to efficiently control which users in an organization can view support cases, which can view, edit, and delete support cases, and which have no access at all. You can configure different levels of Support Center access using Access Groups. Impact: Users with access to IBM Cloud Support Center can create and/or manage support tickets based on their IAM role. Support Center access should be managed and assigned using Access Groups.
input_row.audit: 1. In the IBM Cloud console, go to Manage > Access (IAM) , and then select Access Groups . 2. Look for an access group relating to Support Center. Access Group names are customizable and vary from customer to customer. 3. To verify the access policies for an Access Group, click on an Access Group name and then click Access policies . 4. Check for access policies on the Support Center service.
input_row.remediation: 1. In the IBM Cloud console, go to Manage > Access (IAM) , and then select Access Groups . 2. Select Create Access Group . 3. Give the Access Group a descriptive name, for example, Support Center Viewers or Support Center Admins. 4. Optionally, provide a brief description. 5. Click Create . 6. Once the Access Group is created, click on the Access Policies tab. 7. Click Assign Access . 8. Click on Account Management and select Support Center from the drop down menu. 9. Select the Support Center role(s) that meet your use case. Descriptions are provided for each role in the IAM UI. 10. Click add . 11. Click Assign . 12. Click on the Users tab. 13. Click Add users 14. Select users from the list and click Add to group . Default Value: By default, users do not have access to IBM Cloud Support Center. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/get-support?topic=get-support-access
input_row.impact: Users with access to IBM Cloud Support Center can create and/or manage support tickets based on their IAM role. Support Center access should be managed and assigned using Access Groups.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control involves verifying the existence and configuration of an access group with appropriate IAM roles related to the IBM Cloud Support Center. Given the structured IAM system in IBM Cloud and the ability to use IBM Cloud APIs or CLI to query access groups and their policies, this can be automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_iam_access_group_support_access_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **IBM Cloud API Calls:** Use the `GET /v2/groups` API to list all access groups in the IBM Cloud account.
- **IBM Cloud CLI Commands:** Utilize the `ibmcloud iam access-groups` command to list and describe access groups.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** Access Groups within the IBM Cloud account.
- **Configuration Properties to Validate:**
  - Ensure there is an access group designated for support.
  - The access group should have specific IAM roles (`viewer` or `editor`) for the Support Center service.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** At least one access group is configured with the required roles (`viewer` or higher) for the Support Center service.
  - **Fail:** No access groups are found with appropriate roles for the Support Center.
- **Example:** Use IBM Cloud CLI to verify that an access group has policies granting access to `Support Center` with roles `viewer` or `editor`. This can be further verified by listing the policies associated with the group via `ibmcloud iam access-group-policies <group-id>` command.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The task involves verifying the configuration of access groups and their associated IAM roles, which can be efficiently accomplished using IBM Cloud APIs and IBM Cloud CLI commands. The IBM Cloud IAM API and CLI provide capabilities to list and describe access groups and their policies, making automation feasible.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The use of the `GET /v2/groups` API call and the `ibmcloud iam access-groups` CLI command are appropriate for listing access groups. However, the API endpoint mentioned (`GET /v2/groups`) seems slightly off; the correct endpoint for listing access groups should be `/v2/access/groups`. Additionally, the CLI command `ibmcloud iam access-group-policies <group-id>` is valid for verifying policies associated with a specific access group.
- The program name `ibm_iam_access_group_support_access_verified` is somewhat descriptive but could be improved for clarity and adherence to naming conventions.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **API Endpoint Correction:** Ensure the correct API endpoint is used, which should be `/v2/access/groups` for listing access groups.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming the program to `ibm_iam_access_group_support_center_roles_check` for better clarity and adherence to naming conventions. This name more explicitly describes the specific security check being performed.
- **Additional Considerations:** It may be beneficial to include checks for any custom roles that might be equivalent to `viewer` or `editor` for the Support Center service, as organizations might define custom roles with equivalent permissions.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded, and the technical details are mostly accurate, with minor corrections needed. The use of IBM Cloud APIs and CLI for automation is appropriate and aligns with IBM Cloud's capabilities.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Modify the API endpoint to the correct one (`/v2/access/groups`) and consider updating the program name to `ibm_iam_access_group_support_center_roles_check` for improved clarity and adherence to naming conventions. These changes will enhance the technical accuracy and descriptive clarity of the assessment.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identified that IBM Cloud IAM access groups and their associated policies are fully exposed via IBM Cloud APIs and CLI. The audit steps (listing access groups, checking for policies granting Support Center access) can be performed programmatically using the IBM Cloud IAM API (`/v2/access/groups` and `/v2/access/groups/{group-id}/policies`) and CLI commands. There is no requirement for manual inspection, as all necessary data is accessible and scriptable.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
ibm_iam_access_group_support_center_roles_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- IBM Cloud Resource/Service: IAM Access Groups and Access Policies
- API/CLI Method:  
  - List access groups: `GET /v2/access/groups` or `ibmcloud iam access-groups`
  - List policies for a group: `GET /v2/access/groups/{group-id}/policies` or `ibmcloud iam access-group-policies <group-id>`
- Properties to Check:  
  - At least one access group exists with a policy granting `viewer` or higher role on the `Support Center` service.
- Pass Criteria:  
  - There is at least one access group with a policy for the `Support Center` service and the role is `viewer`, `editor`, or higher.
- Fail Criteria:  
  - No access group has a policy granting the required role on the `Support Center` service.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation, with only minor differences in naming and API endpoint precision. The final program name and API endpoint have been selected for maximum clarity and technical accuracy.

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
